<<Switch to>> <<switch case>> <<for better>> <<efficiency and>> <<cleaner code>> <<when checking>> <<grade values>> <<like A>> <<B C>> <<or D>>.

<<Here is>> <<the optimized>> <<switch variant>> <<of your>> <<original if>> <<statement logic>>:

switch (grade) {
    case "A":
        gradeValue = 4.0;
        break;
    case "B":
        gradeValue = 3.0;
        break;
    case "C":
        gradeValue = 2.0;
        break;
    case "D":
        gradeValue = 1.0;
        break;
    default:
        gradeValue = 0.0;
        break;
}

<<This approach>> <<reduces branching>> <<overhead and>> <<improves readability>> <<for discrete>> <<string values>> <<such as>> <<letter grades>>.
